one_time_description: Specific claim negotiation reimbursement of $290,199 related to treaty benefits claim initiated in 2019 against the Government of Canada
economic_notes: Nation holds 8.33% ownership in Prince Albert First Nations Business Development LP and 8.33% ownership in Prince Albert Development Corporation. Received $50,000 distribution from Prince Albert First Nations Business Development LP. Nation is member of Prince Albert Grand Council (12 Nations). Specific treaty benefits claim initiated in 2019 against Government of Canada with $629,554 in expenses incurred as of March 31, 2022.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2022 — this is a 2022 fiscal year statement, not 2024. Auditor report is dated March 25, 2024 suggesting significant delay in audit completion. Debt covenant violation disclosed in Note 10 — lender waived right to demand immediate repayment. First Nations and Inuit Health Branch shows $0 actual revenue vs $668,328 budget. Consolidated entity includes Shoal Lake Cree Nation Operations and Section 95 Social Housing Operation. Two government business enterprises recorded under modified equity method: Prince Albert Development Corporation and Prince Albert First Nations Business Development LP. No schedule of remuneration provided. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($15,042,078) + PAGC ($1,170,561) + CMHC ($273,975) + First Nations Trust ($197,117) + Nechapanuk ($51,350) + NLCDC ($35,000) = $16,770,081. OSR includes rental revenue, other revenue, specific claim reimbursement, admin fees, expense recovery, and income from government partnerships net of deferral. Capital projects schedule shows $1,782,007 in expenses. Operating fund shows deficit of $4,922,136.
